description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, for 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, for 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. description: Symptom: The switch's connection to Aruba Central fails. Scenario: When disabling and then reenabling the aruba-central location-override setting on the switch, a missing Activate server URL causes the device connection to Central to fail. Workaround: Connect the switch to the Central cluster directly using the central location-override command in the switch command-line interface. Alternatively, remove the central location-override configuration from the switch, ensure that aruba-central is enabled on the switch, and then reboot that device.

description: Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". Workaround: Issue the following commands in order: start-shell; sudo bash; systemctl restart_jitterentropy-rngd; exit; exit; show aruba-central.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, RL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, for 4100i AOS-CX 10.13.0005, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow unsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

description: Symptom: The switch's connection to Aruba Central fails. Scenario: When disabling and then reenabling the aruba-central location-override setting on the switch, a missing Activate server URL causes the device connection to Central to fail. Workaround: Connect the switch to the Central cluster directly using the central location-override command in the switch command-line interface. Alternatively, remove the central location-override configuration from the switch, ensure that aruba-central is enabled on the switch, and then reboot that device.

Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

description: Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". Workaround: Issue the following commands in order: start-shell; sudo bash; systemctl restart_jitterentropy-rngd; exit; exit; show aruba-central.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
